title: Screen form factor, settings surface, and cross-biome progression
summary: Third structured packet for Eco Explorer. This packet is meant to begin after the live world-travel integration lands and the build is clean again. It focuses on the next UX layer the app needs: a wider screen shape, a visible settings/save surface, and clearer collection progress across multiple ecosystems.

execution_order.goal: Adopt a wider rectangular viewport and rebalance all in-canvas safe areas.
execution_order.why_now: The shell now feels game-first, but the visible screen still feels like the old tall slice.

execution_order.goal: Review the widened viewport for readability, hierarchy, and playfield protection.
execution_order.why_now: This catches layout regressions before more UX surfaces are added.

execution_order.goal: Add a visible menu/settings/save surface and persist meaningful player options.
execution_order.why_now: The app already persists state, but it still lacks a player-facing system surface.

execution_order.goal: Review settings, save reset, and menu readability after implementation.
execution_order.why_now: Persistence UX should be checked before it becomes more deeply embedded.

execution_order.goal: Add cross-biome collection progress and richer journal navigation.
execution_order.why_now: Once travel is live, the journal becomes a multi-ecosystem guide and needs clearer structure.

guardrails: Do not fake a wider screen by stretching the old internal viewport with CSS alone.
Treat the post-travel build as the baseline: restore a clean build before starting this packet.
Keep menu and settings wording short, child-friendly, and visually calm.
Preserve existing save data safely when adding new settings fields.
Do not turn progression into a noisy checklist-first system; the journal should still feel like discovery.
acceptance_focus: The app looks wider and more screen-like after travel integration.
There is a visible menu/settings/save surface in the game.
Settings persist safely and reset-save behavior is explicit.
Players can read progress across multiple ecosystems without losing the discovery feel.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, and this packet after main-03 is complete and the build is green. The next phase is screen shape, readable system surfaces, and cross-biome progression clarity.
